name: CryptoPunks
description: One of the earliest NFT projects, predating the ERC-721 standard. Each punk is a 24×24 pixel art image with randomly generated attributes including hairstyle, accessories, and skin tone.
about:
## 10,000 unique collectible characters

Humans have always been collectors, gathering tangible items like gemstones and watches, intangible things like books and art, and abstract concepts like knowledge. CryptoPunks extend this collecting impulse into the digital realm. The project consists of ten thousand 24×24 pixel portraits of punks, each assembled from various individual traits such as hairstyles and jewelry.

## Proof of Ownership on the Ethereum Blockchain

The blockchain establishes a shared historical log and single source of truth about what happened when. This enables tracking ownership of objects over time. When CryptoPunks launched, they came with code processing ownership changes for each punk. All changes must go through this immutable, unblockable program. Anyone can prove punk ownership at any moment.

The Ethereum blockchain is validated by hundreds of thousands of distributed computers globally. Anyone can participate in securing the network. No entity can change any part of its state without following blockchain rules and smart contracts. For CryptoPunks, the relevant code is called the "CryptoPunksMarket" smart contract.

## Inspired the modern CryptoArt movement

The world's biggest artists, auction houses and brands agree we live in a digitized world. This is a new medium, like past art forms, and cryptography and blockchain technologies enable collecting, owning, and trading items on a global, borderless market.

CryptoPunks were the seminal art project of its kind, inspiring various technological standards and spearheading an entire movement. The Punks are now exhibited in prestigious museums like Centre Pompidou and LACMA, celebrated by icons from fashion, music, art and technology.

The CryptoPunksMarket smart contract has processed billions in trading volume. Thousands of accounts have interacted with it, over 6000 have owned punks, and the most traded punk has changed hands 51 times — all handled by code under 200 lines long.

2.5+ billion dollars in secure trading. 200 lines of code. A global community with no middlemen or rent seekers.
risks: No upgradeability — contract bugs are permanent and cannot be patched
Predates ERC-721 — does not implement the standard NFT interface, requiring wrapper contracts for compatibility with modern marketplaces
No royalty enforcement — the built-in marketplace has no creator fee mechanism
ETH withdrawal pattern — sale proceeds and outbid returns must be manually withdrawn via pendingWithdrawals

imageHash.title: The Punks Image
imageHash.description: SHA-256 hash of the composite image containing all 10,000 punks. Used to verify image authenticity.

props.title: The 10,000 CryptoPunks
props.description: The CryptoPunks contract stores a SHA-256 hash of the canonical composite image containing all 10,000 punks. This panel fetches the original image, hashes it in your browser, and checks whether the result matches the onchain value.
props.explainer: If the computed hash matches the onchain hash, the image file is byte-for-byte identical to the one the creators committed when deploying the contract. Any modification — even a single pixel — would produce a completely different hash.

punkIndexToAddress.title: Punk Owner
punkIndexToAddress.description: Look up the current owner of a specific punk by its index.

balanceOf.title: Balance
balanceOf.description: Check how many punks an address owns.

transferPunk.title: Transfer Punk
transferPunk.description: Transfer a punk you own to another address. Does not go through the marketplace.
transferPunk.warning: This is a direct transfer with no payment involved. Make sure you intend to send the punk for free.

punksOfferedForSale.title: Sale Info
punksOfferedForSale.description: Check if a punk is currently offered for sale and at what price.

punkBids.title: Bid Info
punkBids.description: Check the current bid on a punk.

buyPunk.title: Buy Punk
buyPunk.description: Buy a punk that is currently listed for sale. Send ETH equal to or greater than the asking price.
buyPunk.warning: Sends ETH — make sure msg.value meets the asking price.

offerPunkForSale.title: List Punk for Sale
offerPunkForSale.description: List a punk you own for sale on the built-in marketplace at a minimum price.
offerPunkForSale.interpolatedIntent: List Punk #{punkIndex} for sale at {minSalePriceInWei} minimum

offerPunkForSaleToAddress.title: List Punk for Sale (Private)
offerPunkForSaleToAddress.description: List a punk for sale to a specific address only, at a minimum price.

toAddress.label: buyer
toAddress.description: Only this address can buy the punk

fields.label: buyer
fields.description: Only this address can buy the punk

punkNoLongerForSale.title: Delist Punk
punkNoLongerForSale.description: Remove a punk from the marketplace. Cancels the current sale offer.

enterBidForPunk.title: Place Bid
enterBidForPunk.description: Place a bid on a punk by sending ETH. Your ETH is held in the contract until the bid is accepted or you withdraw it.
enterBidForPunk.warning: Your ETH will be locked in the contract until the bid is accepted or withdrawn.

acceptBidForPunk.title: Accept Bid
acceptBidForPunk.description: Accept the highest bid on a punk you own. The punk is transferred to the bidder and the bid amount is credited to your pending withdrawals.

minPrice.label: minimum price
minPrice.description: Minimum bid amount you will accept (protects against front-running)

fields.label: minimum price
fields.description: Minimum bid amount you will accept (protects against front-running)

withdrawBidForPunk.title: Withdraw Bid
withdrawBidForPunk.description: Withdraw your bid on a punk, returning your locked ETH.

pendingWithdrawals.title: Pending Withdrawals
pendingWithdrawals.description: Check the amount of ETH an address can withdraw from sales or returned bids.

withdraw.title: Withdraw ETH
withdraw.description: Withdraw ETH owed to you from punk sales or outbid returns.

getPunk.title: Claim Punk (Inactive)
getPunk.description: Originally used to claim a free punk during the initial distribution. All 10,000 punks have been claimed — this function is no longer usable.
getPunk.deprecated: All 10,000 punks have been claimed. This function will revert.

allPunksAssigned.title: All Punks Assigned
allPunksAssigned.description: Whether all 10,000 punks have been claimed. Returns true.

punksRemainingToAssign.title: Punks Remaining to Assign
punksRemainingToAssign.description: Number of punks still available to claim. Returns 0 (all claimed).

nextPunkIndexToAssign.title: Next Punk Index to Assign
nextPunkIndexToAssign.description: The next punk index to be assigned during the initial claim phase. All punks have been claimed, so this is historical.

PunkTransfer.description: Emitted when a punk is transferred between addresses.

PunkOffered.description: Emitted when a punk is listed for sale.

PunkBidEntered.description: Emitted when a bid is placed on a punk.

PunkBidWithdrawn.description: Emitted when a bid is withdrawn.

PunkBought.description: Emitted when a punk is sold.

PunkNoLongerForSale.description: Emitted when a punk is delisted from the marketplace.

Assign.description: Emitted during the initial claim phase when a punk was assigned to an address.

Transfer.description: Emitted on punk transfer (legacy event, predates ERC-721).
